set_fields.description: Configuring Ralph with command flags is not ergonomic. Add `/ralph configure [task-id]` as the primary UI for inspecting and editing every user-configurable field in Ralph loop state, including capability contracts, pacing/reflection settings, concrete execution contracts, and sandbox settings.
set_fields.design: Add a /ralph configure command. Target resolution: if an id/path is provided, configure that Ralph loop; if omitted, configure the loop owned by the current session; if no current loop exists, show a selector of configurable Ralph loops. Build an overlay or custom UI using Pi TUI components (SettingsList/SelectList/Input/Editor patterns) with sections for: Overview, Pacing (max iterations, items per iteration, reflect cadence), Reflection instructions, Execution contract (view pinned model/thinking/policy and explicit recapture from current session), Tools (searchable multi-select over pi.getAllTools with required Ralph control tools locked), Agents (searchable multi-select over AgentRegistry), and Sandbox (view pinned sandbox and explicit recapture). Writes go through the Ralph configuration service. Make scope explicit in the UI header: `Ralph configure: <task-id>`. Keep CLI flags as shortcuts, not the main UX.
set_fields.acceptance_criteria: `/ralph configure <task-id>` opens an interactive UI for that loop; `/ralph configure` resolves the current Ralph loop or shows a selector; the UI exposes every field classified configurable by tau-o6hb, including max iterations, items per iteration, reflection cadence/instructions, active tool contract, available-tool snapshot/refresh where appropriate, enabled agents, concrete execution contract view/recapture, and sandbox profile view/recapture; required Ralph control tools are displayed as system-managed and cannot be disabled; non-configurable runtime fields are visible as read-only where useful; changes persist to .pi/loops/state/<task-id>.json; unsafe active-child edits show a clear warning; tests cover command target resolution and persisted edits for at least one scalar field plus tools, agents, and concrete execution recapture.
